question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of concurrent gRPC subscriptions with no limit set (limit=0) so that during a network partition the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1))) loop on each subscription never terminates, exhausting all boundedElastic scheduler threads and causing a full service denial for all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition scenario by flooding the gRPC endpoint with subscriptions that each hit retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...)) simultaneously, causing a retry storm that saturates the database connection pool and prevents legitimate subscribers from receiving any messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that timeout(retrieverProperties.getTimeout(), scheduler) is applied after retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...)) to keep a subscription alive indefinitely during a simulated network partition by ensuring each retry completes just within the timeout window, preventing the timeout from ever firing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with startTime set to the minimum allowed value so that during a network partition, each poll() call issues a full-table scan query via topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter), amplifying database load and causing cascading failures across all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=0 (hasLimit() returns false) so that poll() computes pageSize as Math.min(Integer.MAX_VALUE, maxPageSize) on every retry during a network partition, causing each reconnect attempt to issue maximum-size queries that overwhelm the database?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isComplete() logic where throttled=false never returns true unless limitHit, so that during a network partition the unthrottled retriever loops indefinitely up to numRepeats=Long.MAX_VALUE, consuming scheduler threads and database connections without bound?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a subscription with throttled=false and no limit, causing isComplete() to always return false during a network partition, so the repeatWhen loop runs for Long.MAX_VALUE iterations and the service never reclaims the associated resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) configuration so that during a sustained network partition, each of their many subscriptions accumulates exponentially growing backoff timers that hold scheduler thread references, eventually exhausting the JVM's memory through timer object accumulation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with throttled=true and a very large startTime close to the current time, then induce a network partition, so that the retriever's repeatWhen loop with numRepeats=Long.MAX_VALUE keeps polling indefinitely and the timeout(retrieverProperties.getTimeout()) never fires because each poll() returns quickly with empty results?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: PollingContext(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user force the unthrottled path (throttled=false) by manipulating the subscription request, causing PollingContext to set numRepeats=Long.MAX_VALUE and frequency=20ms, so that during a network partition the polling rate is 50x higher than the throttled path and overwhelms the database with reconnect queries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a per-subscriber connection limit so that during a network partition, they open N concurrent subscriptions each triggering retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...)), causing N*retry_count simultaneous database reconnection attempts that prevent the database from recovering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime to a very old timestamp so that during a network partition recovery, poll() returns maxPageSize messages on every retry, causing the total data volume transferred to grow unboundedly and exhaust network bandwidth before the partition is resolved?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onNext(TopicMessage topicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that onNext() increments total and pageSize atomically but isComplete() reads them non-atomically, so that during a network partition with concurrent retries, the completion check races and the retriever continues polling past the intended limit, leaking extra data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a resource leak by opening a subscription and then dropping their client connection during a network partition, so that doOnCancel(context::onComplete) is never triggered because the reactive chain is stuck in retryWhen, leaving the scheduler thread and database cursor allocated indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the jitter(0.1) on the repeatWhen RepeatSpec to desynchronize retry waves across many subscriptions, preventing the server from ever having a quiet period to recover database connections during a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter where limit is set to Long.MAX_VALUE - 1 so that the computation (int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get()) overflows to a negative integer, causing Math.min(limit, maxPageSize) to return a negative pageSize that corrupts the database query during network partition recovery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Schedulers.boundedElastic() shared across all PollingTopicMessageRetriever instances so that during a network partition, their many retrying subscriptions fill all bounded elastic threads, causing legitimate subscriptions' timeout(retrieverProperties.getTimeout(), scheduler) to never fire because the scheduler itself is starved?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1))) has no maximum backoff cap in the retriever, causing each retry to wait exponentially longer and hold a scheduler thread reference for the entire backoff duration during a prolonged network partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger excessive logging by opening many subscriptions that each call onComplete() with a large total count during network partition recovery, causing log flooding that fills disk storage and prevents legitimate error messages from being recorded?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the METRIC constant 'hiero_mirror_grpc_retriever' and the Micrometer observation tap to cause metric cardinality explosion by opening subscriptions with many unique subscriberIds during a network partition, exhausting the metrics registry memory?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime to a timestamp just before a known high-volume period so that during network partition recovery, poll() returns maxPageSize results on every call, causing the Flux.fromStream() to hold a database cursor open for an extended period and block other queries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: retrieve(TopicMessageFilter filter, boolean throttled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...)) and timeout(retrieverProperties.getTimeout(), scheduler) so that a network partition causes the timeout to reset on each retry, effectively making the subscription immortal and never timing out?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/TopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the throttled path's isComplete() check (pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize()) so that during a network partition where the database returns 0 rows, isComplete() immediately returns true and the subscription terminates, causing the user to silently miss messages that arrive during the partition?
